title: Dry Bulk Carrier Newbuilding: Has the Supercycle Run Its Course?
section_path: Dry Bulk Carrier Newbuilding: Has the Supercycle Run Its Course?
section_path_text: Dry Bulk Carrier Newbuilding: Has the Supercycle Run Its Course?
summary:
Main
Image reference: RGB_COLOR_BRS_SHIPBROKERS_MONOGRAM_LOGO.png | assets/2025-08-20_dry-bulk-carrier-newbuilding-has-the-supercycle-run-its-course_img_rgb-color-brs-shipbrokers-monogram-l_ee79606c8e74.png
In the first seven months of 2025, dry bulker ordering activity has experienced an unusually deep slump compared to the last few years. Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-08-19 103538.png | assets/2025-08-20_dry-bulk-carrier-newbuilding-has-the-supercycle-run-its-course_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_a3147569a610.png
New orders for dry bulk carriers plummeted by 63% year-on-year in the first seven months of 2025, totalling only 15.46 million Dwt. This sharp decline stands in stark contrast to the historic peak in 2024, when annual orders hit 63.91 million Dwt-the highest level in the past decade.
